website.text_excerpt: Flare - The Social Network You Listen To Flare Privacy Terms Help Download Voice-first social for real connections The Social App You Listen To. Your Orb knows your friendships. No likes. No scrolling. Just you, your friends, and an AI that knows you. What is Flare? Flare is a voice-first social network. You capture flares — video, photo, voice note, or mood — and three AI agents observe your patterns, surface what matters, and keep you connected with friends. Tap your Aura Orb for a voice briefing about your life and friendships. No likes, no follower counts, no curated content. How it works Capture a flare Video, photo, voice note, or mood. No camera roll. Real-time only. Location is optional. Your Orb learns Three AI agents observe your patterns, surface what matters, and keep you in the loop on friends. Your Orb speaks Tap for a voice briefing about your life and friendships. Bidirectional, ~90 seconds. Why Flare is different - Voice-first - Your Orb speaks to you about your life and friendships. No scrolling required. - Anti-performative - No likes, no follower counts, no pressure to perform for an audience - Three AI agents - Spark, Mirror, and Bond work for you, not engagement algorithms - Proactive, not reactive - AI surfaces connections and insights before you ask, instead of waiting for you to scroll FLARE © 2026 | Privacy Terms Help Blog Supported by

website.title: The Solana Foundation
website.description: The Solana Foundation is a non-profit foundation based in Zug, Switzerland, dedicated to the decentralization, adoption, and security of the Solana ecosystem.
website.text_excerpt: The Solana Foundation The Solana Foundation The Solana Foundation is a non-profit foundation based in Zug, Switzerland, dedicated to the decentralization, adoption, and security of the Solana ecosystem. Learn More about Solana Grants and Funding The Solana Foundation Grants Program is focused on funding efforts to further censorship resistance on the Solana Network Build for the common good Delegation Program Validators can participate in the Solana Foundation Delegation Program if they meet certain performance requirements. Foundation Delegation participants are eligible to receive a delegation from the Solana Foundation, helping decentralize the network. What to expect Censorship resistance, now. The Solana Foundation is working to make the Solana protocol the most censorship resistant network in the world. 1 On the permissionless Solana network, anyone can participate in helping to secure the network as a validator. The Solana Foundation encourages new and existing validators to maintain high quality operations via the incentive structure of our Delegation Program. 2 Community members and token holders can choose to participate in Stake Pools to directly increase the censorship resistance of the network while potentially earning tokens at the same time. website.description: Today, we’re announcing Claude 3.7 Sonnet, our most intelligent model to date and the first hybrid reasoning model generally available on the market.
headings: Claude 3.7 Sonnet: Frontier reasoning made practical
Working with Claude on your codebase
Widening the conversation on frontier AI
KPMG integrates Claude across its core business and workforce of more than 276,000 in strategic alliance
website.text_excerpt: Skip to main content Skip to footer Research Economic Futures Commitments Learn News Try Claude Announcements Claude 3.7 Sonnet and Claude Code Feb 24, 2025 Today, we’re announcing Claude 3.7 Sonnet 1 , our most intelligent model to date and the first hybrid reasoning model on the market. Claude 3.7 Sonnet can produce near-instant responses or extended, step-by-step thinking that is made visible to the user . API users also have fine-grained control over how long the model can think for. Claude 3.7 Sonnet shows particularly strong improvements in coding and front-end web development. Along with the model, we’re also introducing a command line tool for agentic coding, Claude Code. Claude Code is available as a limited research preview, and enables developers to delegate substantial engineering tasks to Claude directly from their terminal. Claude 3.7 Sonnet is now available on all Claude plans—including Free, Pro, Team, and Enterprise—as well as the Claude Developer Platform , Amazon Bedrock , and Google Cloud’s Vertex AI . Extended thinking mode is available on all surfaces except the free Claude tier. In both standard and extended thinking modes, Claude 3.7 Sonnet has the same price as its predecessors: $3 per million input tokens and $15 per million output tokens—which includes thinking tokens. Claude 3.7 Sonnet: Frontier reasoning made practical We’ve developed Claude 3.7 Sonnet with a different philosophy from other reasoning models on the market. Just as humans use a single brain for both quick responses and deep reflection, we believe reasoning should be an integrated capability of frontier models rather than a separate model entirely. This unified approach also creates a more seamless experience for users. Claude 3.7 Sonnet embodies this philosophy in several way...

Teams configure localization engines that persist glossaries, brand voice, and per-locale model chains – then call them from backend code, CLI, CI/CD, or MCP. Terminology stays consistent across every locale, every release. Create localization engine Book a Demo Words processed on Lingo.dev: 2 6 2 , 4 7 1 , 1 6 9 A shift in where the work lives. Localization used to sit outside the product – weeks of coordination, translators new to the domain, fresh context every request. Teams that moved it inside the product found it compounds. Before Translation as a vendor relationship. Requests leave the product. Vendors translate without knowing the domain. Every turnaround arrives fresh, so terminology drifts release over release. Quality lives in reviewer opinions no one can audit. After Translation as infrastructure you configure. A localization engine holds your glossary, brand voice, and per-locale model chain. Every request enters the product with that context already attached. Terminology locks. Quality is scored per dimension and tracked over time. Customers What teams found when they measured. Engineering teams that moved localization inside the product found the context compounds. Terminology locks. Quality becomes measurable. View all customer stories 36 languages automated Our engineers don't even think about localization anymore. They just build features, and translations happen automatically. The barrier to being found hasn't. ProductClank is the coordination layer that lets builders grow without ads, without funding, and without asking permission. Apply to Season 1 Read the manifesto $100K+ distributed to creators 1,000+ campaigns launched 12,383 users onboarded 84% campaign completion The problem The old playbook is broken Technology has made building cheap. The gate is locked from both sides. 01 You need money to promote 02 Investors want traction first 03 Traction needs distribution 04 Distribution needs money ↺ How it works The crowd knows what ads don't When oil prices spike, it's not because a newspaper reported it. It's because thousands of people who know things — traders, suppliers, analysts — acted on what they knew. The price became the news. That's the economist Hayek's insight: when people have skin in the game, markets surface truth faster than any authority can. Polymarket does this for world events Let people put something at stake on an outcome — and the crowd reveals what's actually likely to happen, often before the media does. Not because anyone told them. Because the people with real knowledge had a reason to act on it.
